rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT MILLIE LUONG's Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant Millie Luong's Partial Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is granted in part and denied in part. As to the 6th cause of action (Breach of Contract), the motion is denied. The Second Amended Complaint alleges that Defendants agreed to pay Plaintiff's attorneys fees and costs should he commence litigation after two years in exchange for Plaintiff not filing suit for two years. Promising to pay attorneys' fees and costs is consideration for the agreement. (Second Amended Complaint 49(f).) As to the 7th cause of action (Promise without Intent to Perform), the motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend if Plaintiff can plead facts, in good faith, to establish he sustained damage by waiting two years before filing suit. As to the 9th cause of action (Declaratory Relief), the motion is granted without leave to amend. This cause of action is redundant, as Plaintiff's breach of contract claims have already matured. (See Jolley v. Chase Home Finance LLC (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 872, 909-10, holding where there is "a fully matured cause of action for money, [plaintiff] must seek damages, and not pursue declaratory relief.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. NHUNG T. TRIEU, AKA NICOLE T. TRIEU, AKA TRIFU N. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 3, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff CREDITORS TRADE ASS'N, INC.'S Motion to Compel Answers to Post-Judgment Discovery is GRANTED. Plaintiff CREDITORS TRADE ASS'N, INC.'S request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $300.00. Moving party to provide an Order for the Court's signature, to issue at the time of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL R KURTZMAN et al VS. STEVE SISLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2013, Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANTs MICHAEL KURTZMAN and MEEHYUN KURTZMAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The motion is denied. The fraud in the inducement cause of action supports the prayer for punitive damages. "Although punitive damages may not ordinarily be given for breach of contract, whether the breach be intentional, willful or in bad faith [citations], such damages may be awarded where a defendant fraudulently induces the plaintiff to enter into a contract. [Citations.] The words 'oppression, fraud, or malice' in Civil Code section 3294 being in the disjunctive, fraud alone is an adequate basis for awarding punitive damages. [Citations.]" Las Palmas Assoc. v. Las Palmas Center Assoc. (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 1220, 1239. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MXB HOLDINGS, LP A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al VS. ADAM LEVIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions And Entry Of Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 3, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiff SV ANGEL, LLC'S Motion For Sanctions And Entry Of Default Judgment Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs MXB HOLDINGS, LP, MICHAEL JACKSON, HECKER CONSULTANCY LLC, and SV ANGEL LLC'S Motion for Sanctions and Entry of Default Judgment is GRANTED. Pursuant to Cal. Code Civ. Proc. section 2023.030, the Court finds misuse of the discovery process by Defendants Criterion Capital Partners, LLC, Adam Levin and Tristen Lazareff for failure to respond to discovery, and failure to comply with Orders of this Court. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is DENIED for lack of proof. Moving parties to prepare an order, which shall issue at the time of hearing on the matter. rulings.Case Title: JUAN CHAVARRIA et al VS. BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint. Granted. The proposed cross-complaint arises out of the same transaction as plaintiff's claim. Defendant Bayview Loan Servicing Inc. did not act in bad faith in seeking to file a cross-complaint at this time, and the filing of the cross-complaint is warranted in the interests of justice. rulings.Case Title: ROSA GARCIA VS. OUR VICTORIAN SCHOOLHOUSE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 3, 2013, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT ROSA GARCIA's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The cross-complaint was timely filed because the time runs from notice of entry of order. Causes of action 1 and 2 sufficiently allege the terms of and parties to the contract. The Court overrules as to the 3rd cause of action. It is true that unjust enrichment is not a distinct cause of action, but Plaintiff raised that issue for the first time in the reply papers. The Court overrules as to the 4th cause of action. Paragraphs 27-28 allege that Defendant would not have entered into the transaction but for the mistake. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Wests' Response To Special Interrogatories Set One. Nos 15 And 16 And Motion To Compel Production Pof Documents Request No. 18, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 3, 2013, Line 4: Cross Complainant LAWRENCE EBRINGER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Wests' Response To Special Interrogatories Set One. Nos 15 And 16 And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Request No. 18, Set One Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant and Cross-Complainant LAWRENCE EBRINGER's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Discovery is DENIED, in part and GRANTED, in part. Plaintiffs' responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 15 & 16 are deemed sufficient, and the Motion to Compel further responses to Special Interrogatories is DENIED. Defendant's Motion to Compel the production of documents in response to RFPD No. 18 is GRANTED. Plaintiffs STEPHEN T. WEST and AIMEE G. WEST shall produce all relevant, non-privileged documents in response to RFPD No. 18 on or before October 5, 2013, as previously agreed by the Parties. The time period stated in RFPD No. 18 shall be limited to January 1, 2008 to November 12, 2010. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
